qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in ExecuteCommand node permits arbitrary command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection via the ExecuteCommand node's child_process.exec() usage. The advisory describes AlaSQL SQL injection in the Merge node's 'Combine by SQL' mode. These are different nodes (ExecuteCommand vs Merge), different vulnerable components (child_process.exec vs AlaSQL sandbox), and different root causes (unsanitized command string vs insufficient SQL statement restrictions).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import from URL permits access to internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via the /workflows/from-url endpoint making unvalidated server-side HTTP requests. The advisory describes AlaSQL SQL injection in the Merge node. These are different endpoints, different vulnerable components (axios.get vs AlaSQL sandbox), and different vulnerability classes (CWE-918 SSRF vs CWE-89 SQL Injection / CWE-94 Code Injection).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes Remote Code Execution via AlaSQL SQL injection in n8n's Merge node 'Combine by SQL' mode (CWE-89, CWE-94). Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. The command-injection finding targets the ExecuteCommand node (different node and mechanism), and the SSRF finding targets the workflow import URL endpoint (different endpoint and vulnerability class).
evidence: Advisory root cause: Merge node 'Combine by SQL' mode, AlaSQL sandbox insufficient restrictions on SQL statements
Advisory CWEs: CWE-89 (SQL Injection), CWE-94 (Code Injection)
command-injection-executecommand: targets ExecuteCommand node's child_process.exec() — different node, different root cause
ssrf-workflow-import: targets /workflows/from-url endpoint — different endpoint, different vulnerability class (CWE-918 vs CWE-89/CWE-94)
